data.title: Glassnode: Understanding the future capital trends and bull-bear thresholds of the crypto market
data.description:
Source: Glassnode; Compiled by: Wuzhu, Golden Finance

Summary

  • After Bitcoin broke through $105,000 for the second time in late January, the market entered a contraction phase, with monthly price momentum for major assets falling sharply.

  • Bitcoin remained relatively stable, while Ethereum, Solana, and Memecoins faced deeper adjustments, reflecting changes in risk appetite.

  • Solana has become the market leader in capital inflows over the past two years, while Ethereum has been relatively difficult to attract sustained demand.

  • However, this week, capital flows for all digital assets except Bitcoin fell sharply, and Solana and its related memecoin ecosystem were hit relatively hard.

  • Open interest in perpetual futures fell for Bitcoin (-11.1%), Ethereum (-23.8%), Solana (-6.2%), and Memecoins Index (-52.1%), reflecting a weakening appetite for leveraged speculation.

Market Momentum Takes a Breathing Break

In late January 2025, the Bitcoin market attempted to break through its all-time high and return to the price discovery phase. However, this rally failed to gain the necessary momentum, and the market has since entered a period of contraction and consolidation, with price momentum for major assets falling sharply.

  • Bitcoin: +48.4% (November 2024) → -5.9% (February 2025)

  • Ethereum: +60.3% (December 2024) → -16.9% (February 2025)

  • Solana: +53.2% (November 2024) → -33.1% (February 2025)

  • Meme Coins Index: +90.2% (December 2024) → -37.4% (February 2025)

It is worth noting that Memecoins and Solana thrive in conditions of strong market trends, but also tend to pull back sharply during downturns. Ethereum has been one of the weakest performers throughout the cycle, and while it has outperformed Solana this week, a strong outperformance trend has yet to form.

Assessing Performance So Far

The recent slowdown comes after an extended period of strong growth for the major digital assets. Looking at performance since the beginning of 2023, we can see clear differences in how each asset has performed over the cycle so far:

  • Bitcoin is trading in a range of approximately 3.4x relative to April 2023, providing a benchmark return profile.

  • Ethereum has struggled compared to its peers, with returns ranging between 1.3x and 2.0x relative to April 2023.

  • Solana’s returns since 2023 peaked at 11.8x in early January 2025, but have since fallen dramatically to around 7.6x as the current correction unfolds.

  • Memecoins Index: An explosive price rally in mid-2024 is broadly consistent with Solana’s outperformance, peaking at 5.2x since 2023. However, the sector has been hit quite hard in recent weeks, with overall performance now the worst of the four assets.

This speaks to Solana’s high-beta nature, which has experienced both the strongest upside and the most dramatic pullback. Bitcoin’s more stable performance highlights its resilience as a benchmark return feature in the digital asset space. Memecoins, in particular, have seen a notable drop in investor demand recently, suggesting a shift in risk appetite.

Capital Flows Drive the Market

Solana’s strong performance over the past two years is consistent with sustained capital inflows driven by investor demand. Looking at the monthly changes in realized market capitalization, we can see clear patterns of capital flows across various digital assets:

  • Solana: has been attracting high relative capital inflows, supporting its strong price appreciation.

  • Ethereum: has seen the weakest net capital inflows among major currencies, explaining its relative underperformance.

  • Memecoins:Experienced several sudden but unsustainable surges in capital inflows, reflecting bursts of speculative activity but without sustained momentum.

However, capital inflow momentum has declined across all digital assets in recent weeks. Notably, Ethereum and the top Memecoins have now turned negative (capital outflows), with Ethereum seeing a net outflow of -0.1% in realized market cap, while the Memecoins index saw a more dramatic net outflow of -5.9%.

This indicates a clear cooling of speculative appetite and suggests that capital may flow out of riskier assets in the future.

Futures Market Weakness

As spot market momentum begins to wane, we can also see a decline in capital inflows into the perpetual futures market. Cooling spot demand has led to a sharp drop in perpetual open interest (OI) across all major assets, indicating less speculative activity and lower spot and carry yields.

Over the past 30 days, the rate of change in open interest highlights a general withdrawal of capital:

  • Bitcoin OI: -11.1%

  • Ethereum OI: -23.8%

  • Solana OI: -6.2%

  • Memecoins OI: -52.1%

The overall downward trend in open interest suggests that speculators are reducing leveraged exposure, likely due to weakening market momentum and increased market uncertainty. The biggest decliner was Memecoin, which tends to attract more short-term leveraged bets but quickly loses appeal whenever market sentiment weakens.

Funding Rates Show Bearish Sentiment

The decline in perpetual futures funding rates further exacerbated the weakness in open interest. This reflects a shift in bearish sentiment and the unwinding of leveraged positions, especially in riskier assets.

  • Bitcoin and Ethereum funding rates remain slightly positive, and their deeper liquidity conditions tend to see positive funding rates, except during events when leverage falls sharply.

  • Solana’s funding rates have edged lower and turned negative in recent weeks, suggesting that demand for long speculative positions is cooling.

  • Memecoins’ funding rates have turned very negative, suggesting that shorts now dominate in these highly speculative assets, and many traders are unwinding their positions (or being liquidated).

Negative funding rates for Solana and Memecoins suggest a net turn to bearish sentiment in risky assets, and that excessive long leverage is being unwound.

ETF Flows and Market Impact

Institutional interest in Bitcoin and Ethereum has slowed as the market has been in a contraction phase, based on spot ETF flows. By normalizing net inflows to the native spot volume of each asset, we can measure the weight and impact of ETFs on market dynamics.

  • Bitcoin ETF outflows exceeded $200 million/day last week, but then there was a strong rebound in buy-side activity, exceeding 8% of global spot trading volume, highlighting institutional demand (similar to "buy the dip" behavior).

  • Demand for Ethereum ETFs has cooled significantly and remains much smaller than Bitcoin. ETF activity for ETH is hovering near zero in terms of both net inflows and outflows, indicating a lack of strong traditional investor demand and participation.

This divergence has been a theme of this market cycle so far and has solidified Bitcoin's dominance in institutional portfolios. Ethereum continues to struggle to attract large and sustained inflows, further explaining its relative underperformance in recent years.

Key Bull-Bear Thresholds

Bitcoin is currently trading $1,000-5,000 above the short-term holder (STH) cost basis at $92,500. Historically, this level has been a key pivot point between local bull and bear phases, acting as a pivot point for recent average buyers moving between unrealized profit and loss conditions.

Looking back at previous instances where Bitcoin reached new highs and then corrected downward, we can see similar patterns in May 2021, November 2021, April 2024, and February 2024.

In each case, the downside extended to the lower bound of the STH cost basis model, specifically -1 standard deviation (σ) below the cost basis. Currently, this lower bound is located at $71,600, helping to predict the potential downside risk if these historical patterns repeat themselves.

Consolidation after all-time highs

In previous instances of Bitcoin hitting new highs, we have observed a consistent post-bounce pattern where a surge in realized supply density occurred within the ±15% range of the spot price. This is due to the market shifting from an aggressive uptrend to price consolidation as the market trades in a relatively tight range.

This behavior was evident at previous cycle tops where:

  • As the market rallied within the price discovery range, realized supply density fell all the way to the price peak.

  • Then, as the market enters a correction or consolidation phase, market participants begin to redistribute tokens.

  • This typically occurs close to the STH cost basis as the conviction and demand profile of new investors is tested.

The current actual supply density is consistent with a typical post-ATH consolidation phase, where buyers and sellers attempt to establish a new equilibrium before the next major trend unfolds.

Approaching a Decisive Market Moment

To better understand how sensitive the market is to this supply density, we can analyze the supply held by short-term holders (STHs) during the recent post-ATH distribution phase. This helps gauge the pattern of new investor accumulation and whether the current situation resembles past cycle peaks.

When comparing STH supply changes, we observe:

  • The recent accumulation phase is very similar to May 2021, indicating that supply is equally large and investors are sensitive to price drops below $925K.

  • April 2024 also saw heavy accumulation by new investors, but the magnitude of the STH supply uptrend in the current cycle is structurally more consistent with May 2021 than 2024.

Given these similarities, we are now very close to a decisive moment in the market - a stage where price action is ready to unfold. If demand remains strong, Bitcoin could establish a new range above the ATH. However, a lack of sustained buying pressure could lead to a deeper distribution-driven correction similar to previous post-ATH phases. This is likely driven by panic from recent buyers who are seeing their recently purchased coins go from being profitable to holding unrealized losses.

Conclusion

Bitcoin has been consolidating around $95k for a few weeks now, trading in a relatively stable range. This is not the case for the broader digital asset space, with Ethereum, Solana, and Memecoins all pulling back sharply from their cyclical highs. Demand for Memecoins in particular has clearly cooled, as evidenced by capital outflows, sharp price declines, and bearish sentiment in the futures market.

For Bitcoin, the key level to watch is the short-term holder cost basis around $925k. This is a critical point where the majority of recent buyers’ holdings will turn to unrealized losses. This could lead to more downside as panic sets in. In either case, the current consolidation phase appears to be approaching its late stages, and the market appears ready to move in one direction again.

data.title: SEC Acknowledges Ethereum and XRP ETF Applications, Opening Comment Period
data.description:
SEC Reviews Ethereum ETF Staking Proposal Amid Changing Crypto Stance

The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has officially acknowledged a 19b-4 filing from Cboe BZX Exchange on behalf of 21Shares, seeking approval to enable staking for its Ethereum ETF.

If granted, this would allow the fund to generate additional returns by staking its Ether (ETH) holdings, with those rewards potentially benefiting investors.

According to the filing,

"The ether staked by the Sponsor on behalf of the Trust will consist exclusively of ether owned by the Trust."

It further clarifies that the staking activities "will not constitute 'delegated staking' and will not form part of a 'staking as a service' offering."

🚨JUST IN: The 🇺🇸SEC acknowledges amendment for @21Shares core $ETH ETF staking pic.twitter.com/O0vVXavTwF

— CryptosRus (@CryptosR_Us) February 19, 2025

This proposal follows a similar move by NYSE Arca, which recently filed for permission to stake ETH in Grayscale’s Ethereum ETFs.

Shift in SEC's Approach to Ethereum Staking

The SEC had previously approved spot Ethereum ETFs last summer, but many issuers chose to exclude staking from their registration statements due to regulatory uncertainty.

Under former Chair Gary Gensler, the SEC classified proof-of-stake tokens as securities, leading to concerns about compliance risks.

However, the regulatory stance has evolved under the Trump administration, with the SEC forming a dedicated crypto task force and considering frameworks that could clarify staking’s legal status.

BREAKING: President Trump’s SEC is launching a "crypto task force" to develop clear regulations for the industry. pic.twitter.com/iuM2DIKnbu

— The Kobeissi Letter (@KobeissiLetter) January 21, 2025

Market analysts believe this shift could encourage wider institutional participation in staking.

In December, the YouHodler’s Chief of Markets Ruslan Lienkha, said,

"Under the Trump administration, staking is likely to receive the necessary legal framework, paving the way for broader adoption, including participation by institutions."

Institutional Interest in Ethereum ETFs on the Rise

Ethereum ETFs have seen significant growth in institutional ownership.

Recent 13F filings reveal that overall ETH ETF ownership increased from 4.8% to 14.5% in the fourth quarter, while Bitcoin ETF ownership declined slightly from 22.3% to 21.5%.

This suggests that institutional investors are showing stronger confidence in Ethereum-based products, particularly as staking introduces the potential for additional yield.

SEC Acknowledges XRP ETF Filings, Opening Public Comment Period

In a separate development, the SEC has acknowledged Nasdaq’s filing for the CoinShares XRP ETF, initiating a 21-day public comment period.

This follows Nasdaq’s earlier submission of 19b-4 forms proposing rule changes to list both XRP and Litecoin ETFs.

While this acknowledgment does not guarantee approval, it signals the SEC’s willingness to review crypto investment products more openly than in previous years.

Recently, the SEC has also accepted spot XRP ETF filings from 21Shares, Grayscale, and Bitwise, with applications from Canary Capital and WisdomTree still pending.

🚨BOOOOM: THE SEC HAS JUST ANNOUNCED THEY WILL APPROVE/DECLINE THE #XRP ETF IN DAYS! pic.twitter.com/jPKfQRBqQr

— 25hoursawake (@25hoursawake) February 19, 2025

XRP ETF Approval Odds Increase Amid Changing Regulatory Landscape

XRP, currently priced at $2.69, has climbed the ranks to become the third-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalisation.

XRP is the only top 10 token that has gained value over the past 7 days.

Analysts are cautiously optimistic about the approval of XRP ETFs, with Bloomberg ETF analysts James Seyffart and Eric Balchunas estimating a 65% chance of approval.

Prediction markets, such as Polymarket, place those odds even higher at 78% by the end of 2025.

The SEC’s approach to XRP has evolved significantly since its legal battle with Ripple Labs.

A federal court ruling in August 2024 determined that XRP itself is not inherently a security, though it could be classified as one under specific conditions.

The latest filings suggest the SEC is now referring to XRP as a commodity, aligning its classification with Bitcoin and Ethereum.

Growing Demand for Crypto ETFs Beyond Ethereum and XRP

The rising demand for crypto ETFs extends beyond Ethereum and XRP, with issuers actively exploring new products.

The SEC has already rejected Solana ETF applications from at least two issuers and signaled reluctance toward approving additional crypto ETFs under the Biden administration.

However, with the shifting regulatory environment, new applications for Solana, Litecoin, and Dogecoin ETFs continue to emerge.

Meanwhile, Nasdaq recently listed the Hashdex Nasdaq Crypto Index U.S. ETF, marking the first spot crypto index fund in the country.

🚨 News: #Nasdaq has proposed a new rule to allow listing and trading of digital asset-based investments, including securities and crypto-related #ETFs like the Hashdex Nasdaq Crypto Index US ETF. pic.twitter.com/Cj1MSokCCy

— CoinRank (@CoinRank_io) February 20, 2025

This indicates a broader expansion of the crypto ETF market, with fund managers also experimenting with mechanisms like staking and in-kind redemptions.

Trump Administration's Crypto Policies Influence Market Sentiment

Since taking office, the Trump administration has taken a more crypto-friendly stance, with efforts to position the U.S. as a leader in the digital asset space.

Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse remains optimistic about XRP ETF approvals, citing increased institutional interest and evolving regulations.

As the SEC continues reviewing pending ETF applications, market participants will be watching closely to see how regulators handle crypto investment products in the months ahead.

data.title: Inside Scoop or Keen Eyes? The $100M Trump Meme Coin Whale’s Winning Move
data.description:
Luck Played No Part in Crypto Whale’s $100M Windfall From TRUMP

The trader who turned $1 million worth of Solana (SOL) into over $116 million by jumping on President Donald Trump’s meme coin launch has now been identified, and new insights reveal how he managed to act with such precision.

It was not luck.

On-chain research firm Bubblemaps tracked down the trader as Naseem—a notorious pseudonymous investor and hacker known for token sniping in the Solana community.

1/ How @naseem became the most profitable sniper on Solana, and made over $100M on $TRUMP

A thread with Coffeezilla 🧵 ↓ pic.twitter.com/0UZnJP4WhB

— Bubblemaps (@bubblemaps) February 18, 2025

But an even more intriguing detail emerged: Over two hours before the official announcement of Trump’s meme coin, Naseem attempted to purchase a large amount of the token from the correct contract address.

This raises a pressing question—how could he have known?

In the world of meme coin launches, especially one tied to a US president, this type of early activity raises concerns about potential insider trading.

If Naseem had advanced knowledge of Trump’s token, where did this information come from, and how did he gain access so early?

6/ This address (93Wb) is directly tied to the $TRUMP trade:

• 93Wb sent funds to the same Binance deposit as Split 9
• 93Wb has direct transactions with Split 6 and Split 9

Too many connections to ignore pic.twitter.com/uloBd1eL87

— Bubblemaps (@bubblemaps) February 18, 2025

No Inside Scoop, Just Great Observational Skills

When questioned by Bubblemaps, the trader, Naseem, denied receiving any insider information from Trump’s team.

Instead, he claimed to have independently discovered on-chain evidence pointing to the launch of the president’s token, aided by one of Solana’s largest decentralised exchanges.

This explanation may hold weight.

According to a Bubblemaps researcher, Meteora, a Solana-based exchange involved in the TRUMP token’s launch, interacted with a pool designed to swap TRUMP for USDC a full day before the official announcement.

In doing so, Meteora’s admin account may have inadvertently lent legitimacy to the TRUMP token, which otherwise could have easily been fabricated by any crypto user.

8/ When contacted, @naseem denied any insider knowledge

He shared screenshots showing that he and his crew spotted an official Meteora address interacting with a TRUMP-USDC pool one day before launch

In short, he claims Meteora unintentionally leaked TRUMP… pic.twitter.com/y507JEwCOz

— Bubblemaps (@bubblemaps) February 18, 2025

However, this key detail was buried among over 100 million transactions on the Solana network—an exceedingly valuable insight hidden in a massive digital haystack.

Bubblemaps has yet to find evidence that any other traders noticed and capitalised on this clue ahead of the TRUMP token public launch.

When Naseem tried to purchase TRUMP tokens hours before the president’s announcement, his transaction failed, as the TRUMP/USDC pool had not yet been activated by its admins.

Had the pool been live, would other insiders have seized the opportunity to purchase tokens before the public caught on?

Was Naseem’s success due to sharp observation, or is there a deeper, untold story behind his extraordinary profit?

11/ So the question is — did @naseem and his team really found this information purely on-chain, gaining a strategic and profitable edge?

Or is this a fabricated alibi?

We’ll find out as the story unfolds.

— Bubblemaps (@bubblemaps) February 18, 2025

data.title: Argentina’s President Javier Milei Accused Of Being The Mastermind Behind An Illicit Association That Ochestrated Numerous Frauds
data.description:
President Javier Milei might be the first President to be under scrutiny and now investigation for promoting and misleading his people to purchase a fraudulent memecoin. In a now deleted post, the President shared a link to the Libra project, stating that the project would fund small Argentine businesses and startups.

Argentinian President Javier Milei promoted the $LIBRA shitcoin in a now-deleted tweet, claiming it would 'stimulate growth' in the country. Shortly after his promotion, more than $4.4 billion in market cap was wiped out. The coin was created by the same scammers behind $MELANIA. pic.twitter.com/qtIZVQpiCc

— Pekka Kallioniemi (@P_Kallioniemi) February 17, 2025

Following his social media post, it triggered a massive surge and interest in the coin, leading the coin to shoot up and hit a token market capitalisation of $4.5b.

The token price also soared from almost zero at the time of its launch to nearly $5, according to trading sites. But within hours, Milei deleted his post, causing the token's price of collapse.

A classic rug pull

Investors who have lost millions of dollars from their investment in the cryptocurrency have been enraged by Millei's actions, calling what he did a classic rug pull.

Despite all the evidence that is pointing towards his wrongdoings, President Milei is adamant that he had done nothing wrong. He explained that he was just sharing information through that social media post and he wasn't trying to promote the cryptocurrency.

"I'm not an expert. My speciality is economic growth, with and without money... As a guy who's a super technology enthusiast seeing the possibility of a tool to finance entrepreneurs' projects, I spread the word."

"No soy experto en criptomonedas, lo dice el propio Davis: yo sé de crecimiento económico sin dinero" 🗣️

👀Javier Milei justificó promocionar la criptomoneda $LIBRA afirmando que es un "tecno-optimista"pic.twitter.com/IegGJRGwpk

— El Destape (@eldestapeweb) February 17, 2025

During in an interview, President Milei also said that he acted in "good faith" and dismissed investors' complaints, comparing their action to people who gamble.

"If you go to a casino and lose money, what's there to complain about when you knew the risks?"

In another statement, Milei also attempted to downplay the scale of the damage. According to reports, the President tried to dismiss reports that stated that more than 44,000 investors had suffered losses, claiming the actual number was closer to 5,000.

He also claims that most of the affected investors were from China and the U.S, not Argentina.

LIBRA blaming President Milei for the coin's downfall

The project leaders behind $LIBRA have also blamed President Milei's for the downfall of the cryptocurrency. According to Hayden Davis, a figure linked to the Libra project, President Millei had initially made his endorsement for the project clear, having even ensured the project leaders of his full backing before suddenly backing out-leading to LIBRA's market collapse.

🇦🇷 Argentina President Admits Meeting $LIBRA Team, Denies Endorsement Allegations, which is it?

“As a guy who’s a super technology enthusiast seeing the possibility of a tool to finance entrepreneurs’ projects, I spread the word," Milei said. pic.twitter.com/hFRPqWfKrk

— invΞst00®️ 🏁 (@investinbit) February 19, 2025

But unsurprisingly, Milei has also denied these claims as well. During a televised interview with Todo Noticias on Monday, Milei revealed a shocking revelation that he has been meeting with the creator of Libra, but he has denied any intention of promoting the cryptocurrency for investment.

Shortly after deleting his first social media post, President Milei distanced himself from the project, posting that he had no prior knowledge of the token's details and had decided to stop promoting it after learning more.

Despite his defense, Milei now faces fraud allegations over claims that he promoted a fraudulent cryptocurrency. Over the weekend, local attorneys filed a criminal complaint against him, claiming that the President has was a fundamental participant in the development, execution and planning of the mega-scam related to the launch of the LIBRA token.

#FromTheSouth News Bits | Argentina: The deputies block of "Union Por La Patria" will make official the impeachment request against President Javier Milei for the scandal of the $LIBRA cryptocurrency, promoted by Milei, which resulted in a scam estimated at US$ 87 million. pic.twitter.com/KcE9TPFsy4

— teleSUR English (@telesurenglish) February 19, 2025

Opposition lawmakers are also calling for Milei's impeachment, claiming that this scandal has caused the country embarrassment on an international level. Myriam Bregman, a prominent socialist leader and former Presidential candidate, has also called Milei to answer to the congress and for Milei to be accountable for his actions.

But in a TV interview, Milei appeared combative, stressing that he had "nothing to hide."

data.title: AI Agent, the "short-sightedness" of the crypto world and the "foresight" of Silicon Valley
data.description:
In an article last December, I shared an interview with Virtual Lianchuang by Shenchao. That interview not only deepened my understanding of this project, but also deepened my understanding of the AI ​​agent track.

In the past few months, I have been following the development of the AI ​​agent track, and of course the movements of this team. What is commendable is that no matter how ups and downs the external environment is, the team has been quietly promoting the construction of the project and the construction of the ecosystem.

Such persistence is very difficult.

Recently, a friend recommended to me a recent voice interview with the project Lianchuang. The link to the interview is in the reference link at the end of the article. This Lianchuang is a Malaysian Chinese, and his Mandarin is very good, so I highly recommend that our readers have time to listen to this voice interview.

In this interview, the two views mentioned by the co-founder left a deep impression on me, and today I will share these two views with you.

If we believe that in the future world, the number of AI agents will be greater than that of humans, and AI agents will be “smarter” than humans, then it is entirely possible that Virtual’s economic size will reach or even exceed that of a country.

For the future, the number of AI agents will be greater than that of humans. This is a view I have expressed publicly in my articles a long time ago, and I have no doubt about it. As for AI agents being “smarter” than humans, it goes without saying that I believe that readers who have used some AI tools can experience it for themselves.

The key point of this view is that it envisions a big stage for the future of the AI ​​agent ecosystem: in that future ecosystem, AI agents will trade with each other, communicate with each other, form their own ecosystem, and form their own “society”. They will form another parallel society and construct another parallel economy.

If we further look at the possible quantity and quality of AI agents in the future, this economy will not only exceed the size of a country, I think it is entirely possible that it will exceed the total amount of the current human economy.

If the crypto platform can carry such an economy or even just a part of this economy, the economic value will be unimaginable to us now.

In this regard, Virtual's co-founder expressed optimism about his project and hoped that this platform could become the "country" of AI agents in the future.

Whether the team's dream can be realized or not, whether the project can realize this dream or not, I sincerely applaud this lofty ideal and ambition, and hope that they will persist in working in this direction. Even if the future development of this project is not satisfactory, I hope that the team can find a new direction in the AI ​​agent ecosystem as soon as possible and continue to move forward.

If we understand this idea, we should be more confident in the future and prospects of the AI ​​agent track. If we can have this confidence, then the next point I want to share will be easy to understand.

In today's Silicon Valley, the general view is that the next wave of entrepreneurial track is AI agent. But in the crypto ecosystem, people only regard the AI ​​agent track as a narrative, not a long-term entrepreneurial track.

My previous understanding of this point was obviously short-sighted.

Now I think that Silicon Valley's view is a profound insight.

The next step in the development of AI must be large-scale and wide-ranging applications. And this kind of application must rely on AI agents. And the future AI agents must be spread across all walks of life and penetrate into all aspects of society.

How can such an entrepreneurial opportunity be just a narrative? Just a gust of wind? Just a short-term goal?

But why is the crypto ecosystem, especially the current ecosystem, generally filled with such short-sighted views?

I think this may be related to the composition of participants in the crypto ecosystem.

In the crypto ecosystem, we must recognize that most people in this ecosystem are speculators.

This phenomenon is a fact, but it does not mean that this phenomenon is correct.

But because of this fact, it will lead to most of the opinions we see now are issued by speculators, all of which are clamor, noise and interference. And we who are in this environment will inevitably be affected by this atmosphere and emotions.

I will be more vigilant about this in the future.

Since this is a long-term track, we should pay attention to its development in the long term, and pay attention to the combination and application of various AI agents + Crypto. There must be great opportunities here.

Although the currency prices of various projects in this track have been seriously affected at present, and various so-called insider information has begun to be exposed, these negative information are originally part of an ecosystem, and we should just treat them with a normal mind.

What we need to do is not let these negative information interfere with our forward-looking vision. We should observe the progress of the ecosystem more deeply and pay attention to the teams and projects that are still under construction in the ecosystem.

Miracles are always created by those who insist on building, and those who can really "get rich overnight" must be investors who pay attention to these people and these projects, not speculators who are flooding the market today.

Reference link:

https://www.xiaoyuzhoufm.com/episode/6787b7fcb21f1c0fb3211c5e?s=eyJ1IjogIjYyM2Y0ZGQ2ZWRjZTY3MTA0YTRjMjI2MiJ9

data.title: Binance Stays Radio Silent Despite Being Slapped with $81.5 Billion Lawsuit by Nigeria: When Will the Legal Woes End for the Exchange?
data.description:
Nigeria Slaps Binance with $81.5B Lawsuit

Nigeria’s Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS) has filed a lawsuit against Binance, accusing the cryptocurrency exchange of tax evasion, foreign exchange violations, and destabilising the naira.

The case, lodged at the Federal High Court in Abuja, seeks $81.5 billion—$79.5 billion for alleged economic losses and $2 billion in unpaid taxes for 2022 and 2023—exceeding Binance’s valuation by approximately $20 billion (31.45%).

FIRS claims Binance operated in Nigeria without full regulatory approval and failed to comply with tax obligations, despite maintaining a “significant economic presence” in the country.

The agency is also demanding a 26.75% interest on the alleged back taxes.

Nigerian authorities further allege that Binance’s activities contributed to the naira’s devaluation in foreign exchange markets.

🇳🇬 Nigeria Sues Binance for $81.5 Billion!

⚖️ Nigeria has filed a lawsuit against Binance, demanding:
🔹 $79.5B for alleged economic losses
🔹 $2B in unpaid taxes over two years

This marks one of the biggest legal actions against a crypto exchange.

Could this set a precedent… pic.twitter.com/L8NVnGGIbW

— Blok Topik (@Bok2in) February 19, 2025

This lawsuit follows the detention of Binance executives Tigran Gambaryan and Nadeem Anjarwalla on charges of tax fraud and money laundering, though FIRS later dropped the tax-related charges against them.

Binance Faces Intense Legal Showdown with Nigeria

Nigeria’s lawsuit against Binance underscores stringent regulations imposed on foreign crypto service providers.

The case follows a series of regulatory actions against the exchange, including the unexpected detention of executives Gambaryan and Anjarwalla in February 2024 during what was meant to be a routine meeting with state regulators.

In response, Binance halted all naira trading in March 2024 after officials accused traders on its platform of destabilising the currency.

BOOM 💥

Binance executives Tigran Gambaryan and Nadeem Anjarwalla are detained in Abuja, Nigeria, without knowledge of the investigation, with Gambaryan leading criminaI investigations at Binance. pic.twitter.com/wil8asGcHf

— OKATA 001. (@MbaAdol) March 12, 2024

Gambaryan’s detention drew international attention, particularly after reports emerged that he was suffering from chronic health conditions and was allegedly denied proper medical care.

In July 2024, US Representative Rich McCormick introduced a resolution classifying Gambaryan’s detention as a hostage situation, demanding his release.

Diplomatic pressure intensified in September 2024, with US Ambassador to Nigeria Richard Mills urging the government to free him.

While Binance is still being held in contempt for their crimes in Nigeria, including tax evasion, and money laundering, Gambaryan’s time here was marred by several human rights violations that he is now chasing legal recompense for. pic.twitter.com/pMjAVOFCoS

— Big Tech This Week (@bigtechthisweek) February 17, 2025

Under mounting scrutiny, Nigeria’s Economic and Financial Crimes Commission dropped money laundering charges against Gambaryan in October 2024, leading to his release on 23 October after nearly seven months in custody.

Meanwhile, Binance still faces four counts of tax evasion and civil penalty money laundering charges, which it denies.

The outcome of this case could shape Nigeria’s approach to regulating digital assets and influence the broader crypto landscape in the country.

Binance Goes Radio Silent Against Lawsuit

Binance has repeatedly expressed its willingness to cooperate with Nigerian authorities in assessing potential tax liabilities.

However, the exchange has yet to respond to the lawsuit filed against it.

The community was quick to back the exchange by admonishing the Nigerian government, calling them clowns.

Nigeria wants Binance to pay around $81b in fines and back taxes. The back taxes are denominated in dollars but set at the Nigerian MPR of over 26%. I don’t even know where to start with this stupidity man

— Mayowa A. Balogun (@Sweeegu) February 19, 2025

I'm from Nigeria
And I can single-handedly tell you Nigeria Government just Wang that money,they have no valid claim,they just want another binance employee to show up and detain them for ransom,just like they did with two binance employee that show up the last time.

— Ola justcrypto (@Olajustcrypt0) February 19, 2025

In an affidavit, Jimada Mohammed Yusuf, a member of the Special Investigations Team under the National Security Advisor’s office, accused Binance and its executives of contributing to Nigeria’s economic losses.

He stated that the FIRS had evaluated Binance’s tax obligations for 2022 and 2023 and issued a demand notice—one the company allegedly ignored, leading to legal action.

The lawsuit also claims Binance operated covertly in Nigeria despite its substantial economic footprint.

Additional allegations include violations of Nigeria’s Companies Income Tax Act, the Federal Inland Revenue Service (Establishment) Act of 2007, the Central Bank of Nigeria’s Regulatory Framework for Mobile Money Services, and the Companies Income Tax Significant Economic Presence Order.

Will Legal Woes Continue Mounting for Binance Without Respite?

Nigeria’s lawsuit against Binance marks one of the most aggressive regulatory crackdowns on a crypto exchange.

If the government prevails, Binance could face billions in fines and tighter restrictions.

The $81.5 billion claim dwarfs the $4.3 billion settlement Binance paid to the US Department of Justice for banking violations in December 2023.

Everywhere is silent on Twitter NG as Judge fines #Binance        $4.3 billion in US plea deal after admitting to money laundering & sanctions violations.

Awon werey pic.twitter.com/hZ0Qwyw4Ms

— Ainà Dipo 🇳🇬 (@dipoaina1) February 24, 2024

As the case unfolds, uncertainty looms over Nigeria’s crypto market, leaving traders and investors questioning the future of digital assets in the country.

With mounting legal battles, can Binance withstand the pressure, or is the exchange on the brink of being overwhelmed?

data.title: Understanding the differences between Walrus and existing storage protocols
data.description:
Article source: Four Pillars, compiled by Jinse Finance xiaozou

Mysten Labs co-founder Kostas Kryptos said not long ago: "Walrus is the most modern decentralized hard drive."

‌Walrus is a decentralized storage protocol in the Sui ecosystem‌. In this article, let's explore in depth the differences between this protocol and other existing storage protocols.

Key Points:

  • Mysten Labs has successfully launched Sui Network and DeepBook Protocol in the market, and is now preparing a new initiative - Walrus Protocol.

  • Despite the numerous protocols in the decentralized storage market, Walrus still attracts a lot of attention for two main reasons: 1) its cost-effectiveness and security far exceed existing storage solutions; 2) through Sui Network, stored data can be programmable.

  • Walrus is regarded as the most advanced project among the existing decentralized storage protocols, and its future practicality and value will be highly anticipated.

1Background - Why Walrus? Why now?

After successfully launching the Sui Network and Deepbook protocols, Mysten Labs is now moving into new areas with Walrus. The success of Sui Network and Deepbook has created great expectations for Walrus. However, despite the market's great interest in the Walrus protocol, there are still doubts about it.

These doubts stem from several points: the decentralized storage market has become saturated, many existing solutions are underperforming, and the market is concerned about resource allocation issues - specifically whether Mysten Labs is diverting resources to new projects while continuing to develop and expand Sui Network.

Therefore, we will explore the structure of the Walrus protocol in depth, analyze its differences from existing decentralized storage solutions, and study the relationship between Walrus and Sui Network, focusing on how Walrus integrates with Sui's architecture and enhances the Sui ecosystem.

1.1 Differences between Walrus and existing storage solutions

To understand the necessity of Walrus, we first need to explore its differences from existing decentralized distributed storage solutions. From my point of view, Walrus has three significant differences from existing storage models (especially Filecoin and Arweave):

1.1.1Storage Cost Efficiency

First, Walrus, Arweave and Filecoin have significant differences in storage costs. As Four Pillars explained in their article about Walrus, Arweave requires all nodes to replicate and store all data, while Filecoin allows users to decide how many nodes to store data (users can choose to have only one miner store the data, or to have 100 copies of the data distributed among 100 different miners. Naturally, the more miners requesting to store the data, the higher the cost).

In contrast, Walrus uses Reed-Stuff Encoding, which is significantly cheaper than Arweave and Filecoin, and up to 100 times more efficient (compared to Arweave, which requires the entire network to store data, resulting in a replication cost of up to 500 times, while Walrus only needs 4-5 times replication to maintain efficiency). At the same time, the probability of data loss is also significantly reduced.

In short, Walrus solves the shortcomings of Arweave and Filecoin. Arweave has a low probability of data loss but a high replication cost, while Filecoin provides relatively economical storage based on user needs, but the low-cost option may lead to a higher risk of data loss. Walrus combines the advantages of both, maintaining low replication costs while minimizing the probability of data loss.

In addition, as the number of nodes increases, the cost of Arweave increases linearly because all nodes/designated nodes must store a complete copy of the data. Walrus only requires one network data transfer, and each node stores part of the data. As the network expands, the burden on a single node is reduced. This structural difference makes Walrus's storage cost efficiency significantly higher than Arweave and Filecoin.

1.1.2 Programmability

Although Walrus is superior to Arweave and Filecoin in efficiency, its most significant difference from existing storage models is "programmability". Traditional storage only serves as a simple data warehouse, while Walrus realizes programmable decentralized storage through Sui Network, making its functions go beyond basic data storage.

What if smart contracts can directly reference or trigger data stored in decentralized storage? For example, when minting NFTs, image files can be stored in Walrus and their blob data objects can be created on Sui Network, connecting them to the NFT object. This solves the criticism of the "incompleteness" of traditional NFTs (i.e., tokens are on the chain, while NFT art metadata is stored off the chain), making NFTs through Walrus true Web3 assets.

To take another example directly related to data storage, since Walrus’ blobs can be stored as Sui objects and controlled through Sui’s Move smart contracts, the smart contracts can transfer the stored data to other users or automatically change ownership. This is why we say that data in Walrus is programmable.

In contrast, Arweave and Filecoin have limited or almost impossible dynamic integration with on-chain applications. Although Filecoin has added some smart contract features through FVM (Filecoin Virtual Machine), data modification and control capabilities are still limited, which makes Walrus significantly better than both in terms of programmability.

1.1.3 Data Access and Deletion

A feature of existing storage protocols is that once data is uploaded, it can be accessed by anyone and cannot be deleted. While this feature may be useful for individual users, it has significant limitations for institutions and businesses that need to store sensitive data or need to modify/delete data. In contrast, Walrus allows users to dispose of or modify their data when needed (this is completely different from Arweave, where data cannot be deleted or modified; and Filecoin, where data is deleted not because of the user's will, but because the contract expires or the node storing the user's data goes offline and no longer exists in the network).

Some people may worry that this conflicts with the immutability principle of the blockchain, but remember that only blob data is deleted in Walrus. Independent of the deletion of blob data, transaction data remains immutable, and deleting blob data does not affect the integrity of the blockchain.

Compared to traditional storage, Walrus's enhanced practicality increases its adoption potential in traditional enterprises and Web2 enterprises, and its versatility in the future is highly anticipated.

1.2 Synergy between Walrus and Sui Network

After exploring the differences between Walrus and existing storage protocols, we now look at the relationship between Walrus and Sui Network. When Mysten Labs announced the Walrus protocol, many people were critical, questioning "Should they focus on Sui instead of creating another protocol?" However, as long as you have a basic understanding of how Walrus works, you will understand that Walrus is not a distraction from Sui, but should be seen as a storage stack built to complement the applications on Sui. In other words, not only does Walrus complement Sui Network from a storage perspective, it also has a positive impact on Sui Network’s governance token, SUI, making the two inseparable. Let’s explore this in more detail. 1.2.1 Symbiotic Relationship between Sui and Walrus In fact, Mysten Labs had major concerns about the maintenance and management of storage during Sui’s initial design phase. This is because the state of the blockchain inevitably grows with increasing usage, which could eventually lead to increased transaction fees for future Sui Network users. Therefore, from Sui’s early design phase, Mysten Labs introduced a unique concept of a storage fund to address Sui’s storage challenges.

Sui Storage Fund operates as follows: Fees submitted by users to Sui validators are divided into 1) gas fees associated with computation and 2) storage costs for data storage. Sui charges users storage costs for permanent data storage upfront and pools these funds into the Storage Fund. The Storage Fund continuously redistributes accumulated amounts to validators while the data is still stored on-chain. Additionally, if users delete data, they can receive a refund for storage costs.

Sui’s unique on-chain data storage system produces two results:

1) Users receive rebates on storage costs when they delete on-chain data, which creates an economic incentive to reduce the capacity of the distributed ledger.

2) The system solves the sustainability issues associated with storage by collecting storage fees upfront and using them as an incentive for future validators.

While Sui solves the sustainability issue through this unique structure, storing large blobs of data (such as media files) on-chain is still burdensome. This is where Walrus comes in - by storing large blobs of data with Walrus while objectifying the metadata of the blobs on Sui for control, data can be programmable without being stored directly on Sui.

In addition, through Sui, Walrus achieves the most significant feature compared to other storage protocols, that is, the stored data is programmable and controllable. Ultimately, Sui and Walrus form a symbiotic relationship, which creates unique advantages and complements each other's limitations.

1.2.2 Walrus makes SUI tokens a deflationary asset

As shown in the storage fund example, Sui Network requires a certain amount of Sui tokens to be paid as the storage cost of storing any object. Walrus is no exception. When a blob data object is created in Walrus, a number of Sui tokens proportional to the size of the object are locked in the storage fund.

While some of the fees are refundable when data is deleted, some of the fees create a burn effect by permanently removing tokens from circulation. In other words, the more data is stored through Walrus, the more Sui tokens are permanently locked in the storage fund, creating a virtuous cycle where increased Walrus usage leads to a decrease in Sui supply.

In summary, the emergence of Walrus is good news for Sui Network, both from a network and asset perspective, and it is expected that the Sui ecosystem will develop in a more diversified direction through Walrus.

2Conclusion - Walrus will become Sui's most critical protocol

2.1 Mysten Labsis not just building a blockchain

When Mysten Labs was first established, I thought it was just a company focused on Sui Network. However, after witnessing the launch of services such as Deepbook and Sui Naming Service, I began to think about what Mysten Labs's vision is. When I came into contact with Walrus, I came to the conclusion that their goal is to build a complete Web3 decentralized infrastructure.

Mysten Labs has a different time horizon than other companies when looking at this industry. They didn’t just issue a token, create hype, and sell off; instead, they had a vision to lead innovation in all areas of execution, storage, consensus, and communication, while understanding the inertia of users accustomed to Web2 services and building the most appropriate user interface for them.

Execution and consensus are handled by Sui Network (with ongoing development through initiatives like Mysticeti and Pilotfish & Remora), storage is handled by Walrus, communication is handled by SCION (a next-generation internet architecture that protects network packets from DDoS and routing attacks - note that SCION was not created by Mysten Labs, but is mentioned because it will be used in Sui Network), and the Web2-familiar user interface is managed by zkLogin, Stashed, SEAL, and KELP.

If all of these initiatives can successfully take root, I believe Mysten Labs can rewrite the existing Web3 paradigm. My initial thoughts were limited. Mysten Labs is not just trying to build a blockchain - they are a team building infrastructure for a new web. Of course, I believe Sui is the core of Mysten Labs's vision, and the other initiatives all play a complementary role. The same is true for Walrus, which in my opinion is probably the most important protocol among them.

2.2Walrus is not limited to the Sui ecosystem

However, Walrus is not limited to the Sui ecosystem. Like other storage protocols, Walrus can be used by any third party outside of the Sui application, and it may even become a useful alternative to existing storage protocols or other DA layers such as Celestia, EigenDA, Avail. This usability of Walrus extends the demand for Sui tokens beyond the Sui Network. When Walrus is used, objects are created on the Sui Network, resulting in a reduction in the Sui supply. In other words, Walrus has the potential to make Sui tokens a more attractive asset by creating external demand (this is not an investment recommendation, but a structurally possible scenario). Therefore, Walrus is expected to become a bridge for Sui to expand in multiple directions.

2.3 Can Walrus surpass Filecoin?

While comparing the value between specific protocols is a delicate question, I am very optimistic about the future of Walrus because: 1) its operating mechanism is far more efficient than existing storage protocols; 2) it can perform tasks that existing storage protocols cannot (becoming a DA or making stored data programmable); 3) it already has a solid network and user base through Sui Network.

If Walrus becomes not only the storage layer of Sui Network, but also the representative storage protocol of Web3 as envisioned by Mysten Labs, it has the potential to become the leading protocol in the storage field.

data.title: Nigeria files $81.5 billion lawsuit against Binance
data.description:
Author: Vince Quill, CoinTelegraph; Translator: Baishui, Golden Finance

The Nigerian government has filed an $81.5 billion lawsuit against Binance, the world's largest centralized exchange, demanding it to pay $2 billion in back taxes and $79 billion in economic losses because Binance allegedly devalued Nigeria's local legal currency.

According to Reuters, Nigeria's Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS) said Binance has a "significant economic presence" in Nigeria and owes taxes for 2022 and 2023. FIRS also reportedly demanded that the company pay 26.75 percent interest on the taxes owed and went on to claim that the exchange played a major role in reducing the value of the Nigerian naira in the foreign exchange market.

The West African country previously charged and detained two Binance executives, Tigran Gambaryan and Nadeem Anjarwalla, on charges of tax fraud and money laundering. FIRS later dropped the tax charges against the two executives.

Since 2019, the Naira M2 money supply has increased significantly. Source: Trading Economics

Nigeria and Binance are embroiled in a high-profile legal dispute

In February 2024, the Nigerian government unexpectedly detained Gambaryan and Anjarwalla during routine talks between the company and national regulators.

Binance halted all naira trading activity on its platform in March 2024 after the two executives were detained and Nigerian officials complained that traders on the platform were destabilizing the naira.

Gambaryan’s detention attracted media attention amid reports that he suffers from a chronic illness but was reportedly denied adequate medical care.

In July 2024, U.S. Representative Rich McCormick introduced a resolution that classified the detention of American citizen Gambaryan as a hostage situation and called for the executive's immediate release.

Gambaryan sat in the courtroom, visibly shocked by the trial process and the poor prison conditions. Source: Associated Press

U.S. diplomats have stepped up pressure on the Nigerian government to release the Binance executive in September 2024, including a call from U.S. Ambassador to Nigeria Richard Mills to free Gambaryan .

Following a pressure campaign, the country’s Economic and Financial Crimes Commission dropped money laundering charges against Binance executives in October 2024.

Gambaryan was released from Nigeria on October 23, 2024, after spending nearly seven months in prison and a complex legal battle.

data.title: Musk's xAI launches "the most powerful AI on Earth"
data.description:
xAI, Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company, unveiled its latest AI chatbot, Grok3.

Grok3 is a major leap forward in science, math, and creativity compared to its predecessor.

During the live demonstration, Musk highlighted the chatbot’s dramatic progress, saying, “We’re very excited to show you Grok3, which has leapfrogged its capabilities in a short period of time over the previous generation, Grok2.”

The event attracted nearly 100,000 viewers online, demonstrating the huge popularity of Grok3. Musk also thanked the development team for their hard work: "I feel very honored to work with such an excellent team."

01. The name "Grok" and its meaning

Musk also explained the origin of the name "Grok", saying that it was inspired by Robert Heinlein's science fiction novel "Stranger in a Strange Land".

In the novel, a character who grew up on Mars used the word "grok", which means to fully and deeply understand something.

“Empathy is an important part of understanding,” Musk explained. This is also the core quality that Grok3 pursues - establishing an emotional connection based on deep understanding.

02. Grok3 Development Process

Developing Grok3 was not easy. xAI executives talked about the huge challenges of building the infrastructure required to support such an advanced artificial intelligence system.

“We were on a tight schedule because we wanted to launch Grok3 as soon as possible,” said a team member.

They mentioned that they decided to build their own data center and completed the deployment of the first 100,000 GPUs in just 122 days, setting an industry record.

But the work did not stop there. In the following 92 days, the size of the H100 GPU cluster doubled, and the computing power was significantly improved, providing strong support for the continued optimization of Grok3.

“We’ve used this computing power to continuously improve our products along the way,” executives said.

03. Creative AI that “thinks”

One of the most striking features of Grok3 is its creativity.

During the demonstration, this AI showed amazing creative talent—it cleverly combined elements of Tetris and Puyo Puyo to design a new game.

This not only demonstrates Grok3’s strength in the field of technology, but also proves its unlimited potential in creative fields such as entertainment and art.

The xAI team said that Grok3 will be updated daily to ensure that it is always at the forefront of technology. This continuous optimization and upgrade mechanism allows Grok3 to continuously break through itself and maintain its leading edge.

In the future, Grok3's creative ability will not only be limited to game design, it may also bring revolutionary changes to film script creation, music production, art design and other fields.

With the continuous advancement of technology, this "thinking" AI may redefine human cognition of creativity.

04. Model and Performance

Grok3 is not a single model, but a family of models, each with its own unique features.

Grok3 mini is a streamlined version of the model that provides faster responses, but may compromise accuracy.

The other two versions, Grok3 Reasoning and Grok3 mini Reasoning, are designed to carefully "think" about the problem before giving a solution.

Users can use these reasoning models to solve complex problems through the Grok application.

xAI claims that Grok3 beat GPT-4o in benchmarks such as AIME and GPQA. Among them, AIME mainly tests the model's performance on mathematical problems, while GPQA focuses on doctoral-level scientific problems.

Early versions of Grok3 also achieved competitive results in Chatbot Arena, a crowdsourced testing platform that compares multiple AI models based on user-preferred answers.

In addition, xAI said that Grok3 Inference beat the highest version of o3-mini, o3-mini high, in multiple popular benchmarks.

05. Future Updates

Musk announced that Grok3 will launch a "voice mode" in the coming weeks. The model will also be integrated into xAI’s enterprise API via the DeepSearch feature.

Grok3 will initially be available to Premium Plus subscribers of the X platform.

A new subscription service called SuperGrok has also been launched for the Grok.com website and the Grok mobile app.

In a few months, xAI plans to open source Grok2, following their usual practice of open sourcing the previous version after the new version is fully launched.

Original source:

1.https://www.cnbctv18.com/technology/grok-3-launch-updates-elon-musks-smartest-ai-on-earth-x-users-check-details-here-19560488.htm

2.https://www.newsbytesapp.com/news/science/elon-musk-s-ai-firm-xai-unveils-advanced-model-grok-3/story

The Chinese content is compiled by the MetaverseHub team. Please contact us if you need to reprint.

data.title: Binance sued in Nigeria; MiCA approves 10 stablecoin issuers
data.description:
Headlines

▌Binance is sued in Nigeria and asked to pay up to $81 billion in fines

Nigerian authorities sued cryptocurrency exchange Binance for more than $79 billion in economic losses and $2 billion in back taxes. The amount is almost 19 times the $4.3 billion fine Binance paid in 2023 after admitting to violating U.S. banking laws. The Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS), the Nigerian tax authority, also reportedly claimed that Binance was operating illegally in the country.

▌EU MiCA approves 10 stablecoin issuers, Tether is not on the list

The list of compliant stablecoin issuers under the EU Crypto Asset Market Regulation Act (MiCA) was announced, and 10 institutions including Circle, Crypto.com, and Societe Generale were approved to issue euro and dollar stablecoins, involving a total of 10 euro stablecoins and 5 dollar stablecoins. The world's largest stablecoin issuer Tether (USDT, with a market value of over $141 billion) did not appear on the list.

    Market

    As of press time, according to Coingecko data:

    BTC's latest transaction price is $96,616.72, with a daily increase or decrease of +1.1%;

    ETH's latest transaction price is $2,715.90, with a daily increase or decrease of +1.7%;

    BNB's latest transaction price is $651.11, with a daily increase or decrease of +0.6%；

    SOL's latest transaction price is 169.03 US dollars, with a daily increase or decrease of -0.3%；

    DOGE's latest transaction price is 0.2549 US dollars, with a daily increase or decrease of +1.3%；

    XPR's latest transaction price is 2.74 US dollars, with a daily increase or decrease of +6.8%。

    Policy

    ▌US President Trump: Will announce tariffs on cars, semiconductors, chips, pharmaceuticals, and possibly wood within a month

    US President Trump said that he would announce tariffs on cars, semiconductors, chips, pharmaceuticals, and possibly wood within the next month or earlier.

    ▌Trump: The Biden administration’s war on Bitcoin and cryptocurrency has ended

    US President Trump said that I have signed an executive order to put the United States at the forefront of artificial intelligence and end the Biden administration’s war on Bitcoin and cryptocurrency, marking the advent of a new era of digital assets.

    ▌President of The ETF Store: The US SEC has recognized the 19b-4 document submitted by Cboe

    Nate Geraci, president of The ETF Store, said on the social platform that the SEC has recognized the 19b-4 document submitted by Cboe, which seeks approval for the pledge function in the 21Shares Core Ethereum ETF.

    ▌Trump: Consider distributing 20% ​​of the funds saved by the Department of Government Efficiency to Americans

    US President Trump said that he is working hard to balance the budget immediately. I hope to achieve a balanced budget, but I don’t want to make a commitment now. Trump said that he is considering distributing 20% ​​of the funds saved by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to Americans, and he may also use 20% of the funds saved by DOGE to repay debts.

    ▌U.S. Department of Justice: Prosecutors file civil forfeiture lawsuit against a crypto investment fraud case to recover crypto assets

    According to the official website of the U.S. Department of Justice, U.S. prosecutors filed a civil forfeiture lawsuit against a crypto investment fraud case to recover 7.24 BTC, 14,120 USDT, 105.75 ETH and other crypto assets. The investigation showed that a Massachusetts resident was lured into a fake investment group and was deceived into transferring funds to a suspicious trading platform. Law enforcement agencies found that at least three victims in the United States remitted money to the same platform, and some of the funds were seized in October 2024. The prosecutor emphasized that the use of telecommunications means to defraud or conceal criminal proceeds are federal crimes, and the relevant assets will be returned to the victims after the civil lawsuit is concluded.

    ▌U.S. SEC Solicits Public Comments on WisdomTree XRP ETF Proposal

    The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) formally accepted the WisdomTree XRP ETF application on Wednesday and solicited public comments. The SEC requires relevant comments to be submitted within 21 days of publication in the Federal Register. The agency may decide to approve, reject or "initiate further review procedures."

    ▌Montana Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Legislation Has Passed Committee Review and Is About to Enter the House of Representatives Voting Stage

    According to market news, Montana's Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Legislation has passed committee review and is about to enter the House of Representatives voting stage.  

    As previously reported, on February 9, Montana House Bill No. 429 will authorize the state treasurer to invest up to $50 million from the general fund in "digital assets with a market value of more than $750 billion," namely Bitcoin, by July 15, 2025. These investments must be held by a qualified custodian or made through a trading platform trading fund.

      Blockchain Applications

      ▌FOX reporter: Canary Capital launches AXL Trust, the first trust to provide blockchain interoperability investment

      FOX Business reporter Eleanor Terrett shared in a post that Canary Capital launched the AXL (Axelar) Trust Fund, Canary AXL Trust, which will contain the native token of the Axelar network and is the first investment trust to provide general blockchain interoperability protocol investment.  

      - Axelar Protocol has emerged as a leading cross-chain protocol and is working closely with Ripple to support interoperability of its stablecoin RLUSD.  

      - Axelar acts as a bridge between the XRP Ledger and the XRP Ledger EVM, an Ethereum-compatible network.  

      - The Trust will provide institutional investors with exposure to blockchain interconnected technologies, connecting Web3 ecosystems such as XRP Ledger, Hedera, Stellar, TON, Sui, Solana and Bitcoin.

        Cryptocurrency

        ▌Coinbase CEO: I am not a Meme coin trader, and Coinbase adheres to the "free market" principle in listing coins

        Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong expressed his views on the Meme coin craze on the X platform. He said that although he is not a Meme coin trader, he believes that Meme coins have become a phenomenon that cannot be ignored in the field of cryptocurrency. From the early Dogecoin to the various Meme coins today, although these tokens seem to be "toys" at the beginning, they may evolve into more powerful tools over time.  

        Regarding Coinbase's listing standards, Brian Armstrong said that from the first principle, we believe in free market capitalism. If our customers want it and it is legal, our goal is to let them make their own choices. But our job is to provide them with the best information we can find so that they can make an informed choice. If the token is a scam or fraud, we will delete it. But if it's just low quality, customers should be able to see the review or community notes and make their own choice. We want to provide consumers with useful information, not play judge and jury, or endorse/recommend investments.

        ▌Strategy has about 1,400 institutional shareholders holding about $34 billion worth of MSTR shares

        While Michael Saylor-led Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) accelerated its purchases of Bitcoin in the fourth quarter of 2024, institutional investment managers were also aggressively increasing their holdings of MSTR shares.  

        Institutional investment managers who manage at least $100 million in equity assets continued to increase their holdings of MSTR in the fourth quarter, according to the latest 13F report filed. Fintel data shows that Strategy currently has about 1,400 institutional shareholders holding about $34 billion worth of MSTR shares. Vanguard, the world's second-largest asset manager, sold about 1.5 million shares of MSTR between the third and fourth quarters, but still ranked as the largest shareholder with a position of 16.3 million shares. Capital International Investors held 12.5 million shares, followed by BlackRock with 11.2 million shares.  

        It is worth noting that institutions also hold nearly $37 billion worth of MSTR options, doubling from the previous quarter. Susquehanna International Group holds 16.8 million put options, followed by Citadel Advisors with 13.6 million put options. Meanwhile, Jane Street holds 13.6 million call options, showing a more optimistic market expectation.

        ▌Billionaire Mark Cuban suspends Meme coin plan, saying major changes are needed

        Billionaire Mark Cuban announced the suspension of Meme coin plan, saying major changes are needed.  

        On January 21, Mark Cuban wrote: "If Meme coins are feasible, maybe I will issue one. Same terms as TRUMP, 20% circulating supply, same release schedule. There is one difference, that is, all revenue from the sale of tokens goes to the U.S. Treasury. The wallet address will be published so that everyone can track it. If you want to gamble, come on. But at least use it to reduce U.S. debt."

        ▌Institutional Bitcoin spot ETF holdings in Q4 2024 increased by more than 200% compared with Q3

        According to the 13F filing data submitted by the institution to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), large institutions such as pension funds or hedge funds tripled the size of spot Bitcoin ETFs held in the fourth quarter of 2024.  

        As of Q4 2024, institutional investors held $38.7 billion worth of spot Bitcoin ETFs. That’s more than three times the amount reported in Q3, which reported just $12.4 billion in holdings.  

        Among them, the Wisconsin Investment Board increased its holdings of BlackRock iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) to just over 6 million shares. Billionaire hedge fund investor Paul Tudor also nearly doubled his stake in IBIT from 4,428,230 shares to 8,048,552 shares.  

        IBIT now has 1,100 institutional holders who have reported their holdings through 13F filings, according to Eric Balchunas, senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence. Most newly launched ETFs typically have fewer than 10 institutional holders.

        ▌Vitalik Buterin: Ethereum is not a commercial company. If Ethereum becomes a company, it will lose its meaning of existence

        In a text AMA held on the Tako platform, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin responded to the question "Do you think Ethereum needs more commercial management?" He said: "I think Ethereum is a decentralized ecosystem, not a company. If Ethereum becomes a company, we will lose most of the meaning of Ethereum's existence. In fact, there are many large companies in the Ethereum ecosystem: Consensys, various client teams (Nethermind, Nimbus, etc.), Coinbase, L2 teams (including Aztec and intmax, their privacy technology is very interesting and underestimated by many people). The best way is to find ways to give these companies more opportunities to realize the advantages of the company, and the foundation plays a coordinating role. ”

        Important Economic Dynamics

        ▌Fed Bostic: Confidence in the economic outlook for 2025 has decreased

        Fed Bostic said that confidence in the economic outlook for 2025 has decreased. It is difficult to take all potential policy changes into account in the economic outlook; inflation progress is not expected to be a straight line. The Fed did not cut interest rates significantly last year, and the policy remains restrictive; it is satisfied with the suspension of interest rate cuts to observe economic development. The Fed is approaching the threshold level of its balance sheet, and the debt ceiling is a factor in determining this; it hopes to pay attention to the balance sheet on a daily basis to ensure that asset shrinkage is not excessive.

        ▌The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in March is 97.5%

        According to CME's "Fed Watch": The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in March is 97.5%, and the probability of a 25 basis point rate cut is 2.5%. The probability of maintaining the current interest rate unchanged by May is 88.9%, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut is 10.8%, and the probability of a cumulative 50 basis point rate cut is 0.2%.

        ▌Fed Vice Chairman Jefferson: Monetary policy remains restrictive

        Fed Vice Chairman Jefferson said that monetary policy remains restrictive. The US economy is performing quite strongly; the US labor market is solid, inflation has slowed but is still high, and the road back to the 2% inflation target may be bumpy; the Fed can wait when considering the next monetary policy; the Fed's interest rate cut is reducing actual borrowing costs.

        ▌The three major U.S. stock indexes all closed slightly higher

        The three major U.S. stock indexes all closed slightly higher, with the S&P 500 up 0.24%, continuing to hit a record high; the Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.16%, and the Nasdaq up 0.07%. Intel fell more than 6%. Palantir fell about 10%, the biggest single-day drop since August last year.

        ▌Analysts: Under many influencing factors, U.S. stocks still tend to be positive

        On Wednesday, U.S. stocks fluctuated between slight gains and slight losses as investors analyzed the minutes of the Federal Reserve's January policy meeting and digested the tariff measures announced by Trump. The S&P 500 is expected to set a record closing high for the second consecutive day, the Nasdaq was basically flat, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell slightly. "The market is still leaning toward the positive side in the face of high valuations, lower first-quarter earnings expectations and uncertainty about global trade," said Sam Stovall, chief investment strategist at CFRA Research in New York. Policymakers expressed concerns about stubborn inflation and the impact Trump's policy proposals, especially tariffs, could have on their efforts to reduce price growth to their targets after minutes from the Fed's January meeting showed. "The Fed will continue to be data-dependent and they are in no rush to cut rates. (There's really) nothing new, but at the same time there's nothing unexpectedly negative," Stovall added.

        ▌Fed Minutes: Want to see further progress on inflation before cutting rates

        The Fed wants to see "further progress on inflation" before deciding to cut rates again, according to the minutes. The Fed held interest rates steady in a range of 4.25%-4.5% at its last meeting, citing rising inflationary pressures, and markets expect no rate cut at its March meeting either. According to the minutes, the committee unanimously agreed that "the Federal Reserve has ample time to assess the changing outlook for economic activity, the labor market, and inflation." Many economists and market strategists worry that tariffs and stricter immigration policies could increase inflationary pressures, potentially offsetting the positive effects of lower taxes and looser regulations. The Fed has previously indicated that it is in no hurry to cut interest rates further due to persistently high inflation. Inflation has retreated from its post-pandemic highs but remains stubbornly above the Fed's ideal. For example, the consumer price index rose 3% year-on-year in January, the fastest pace in seven months and above the Fed's 2% target.

                  Golden Encyclopedia

                  ▌What is Fractal Bitcoin

                  Fractal Bitcoin is an innovative blockchain expansion solution based on recursive virtualization technology. It enhances the scalability of Bitcoin by creating a multi-layer network structure while maintaining a secure connection with the main network. The creation background of Fractal Bitcoin stems from the emergence of the Ordinals protocol and related applications in 2023. For security reasons, Bitcoin has imposed restrictions on opcodes and block storage space in its iterations. When the Bitcoin chain ecosystem develops rapidly, there are limitations in chain computing power and block space, so developers have proposed the concept of fractal Bitcoin, hoping to solve the shortcomings of the Bitcoin network.
